name: mersen
tagline: Advanced Materials and Electrical Power Solutions
description: Mersen is a global expert in electrical power and advanced materials, providing innovative solutions for electrical protection, power management, and high-performance materials.
longDescription: Mersen is a global leader in electrical power and advanced materials, serving diverse industries with innovative solutions for over 130 years. The company specializes in electrical protection devices including high-speed fuses, DC fuses, and surge protection; power management solutions for power electronics; and advanced materials including graphite, carbon, and refractory metals. Mersen operates manufacturing facilities and commercial offices worldwide, providing local support to customers in electric vehicles, renewable energy, semiconductors, and industrial applications. With a strong commitment to innovation and quality, Mersen holds numerous international certifications and patents in electrical protection technology.

seoTitle: Mersen Fuse Distributor | High-Speed, DC, Industrial Protection
seoDescription: Authorized Mersen distributor providing high-speed fuses, DC fuses, and surge protection devices. Technical support and competitive pricing for EV, solar, and industrial applications.

faqs.question: Is LiTong an authorized distributor of Mersen products?
faqs.answer: Yes, LiTong Electronics is an authorized distributor of Mersen electrical protection products. We maintain strategic inventory of Mersen high-speed fuses, DC fuses, and surge protection devices. As an authorized distributor, we provide genuine products with full factory warranty, technical support from certified FAEs, and competitive pricing. Our partnership with Mersen enables us to offer fast delivery, application engineering support, and access to the latest product releases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team to verify current inventory and pricing for your specific Mersen fuse requirements.

faqs.question: What types of fuses does Mersen manufacture?
faqs.answer: Mersen manufactures a comprehensive range of electrical protection devices: 1) High-Speed Fuses - Ultra-fast acting fuses for semiconductor protection in power electronics, featuring low I2t values and high breaking capacity; 2) DC Fuses - High-voltage DC fuses for electric vehicles (up to 1000VDC), energy storage systems (up to 1500VDC), and solar applications; 3) Industrial Fuses - Power and distribution fuses for industrial applications including motor protection and power distribution; 4) Surge Protection Devices - SPDs for protecting electrical equipment from voltage surges and lightning strikes. All products comply with international standards including UL, IEC, and CE.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right Mersen protection device for your application.

faqs.question: How do Mersen high-speed fuses protect semiconductors?
faqs.answer: Mersen high-speed fuses are specifically designed to protect power semiconductors (IGBTs, diodes, thyristors) from overcurrent damage. Key features include: 1) Ultra-fast acting - Opens within milliseconds to limit let-through energy (I2t); 2) Low I2t values - Minimizes thermal stress on protected semiconductors; 3) Current limitation - Reduces peak fault current to safe levels; 4) High breaking capacity - Safely interrupts high fault currents up to 200kA; 5) Arc voltage control - Limits arc voltage to protect semiconductor junctions. The fuse acts faster than the semiconductor's thermal time constant, preventing device destruction during short circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Mersen high-speed fuses for all semiconductor protection applications. Contact our FAE for fuse selection based on your semiconductor ratings.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Mersen high-speed fuses and standard fuses?
faqs.answer: Mersen high-speed fuses differ from standard fuses in several critical aspects: 1) Opening speed - High-speed fuses open in <1ms vs 10-100ms for standard fuses; 2) I2t let-through - High-speed fuses have much lower I2t values to protect semiconductors; 3) Current limitation - High-speed fuses actively limit peak fault current; 4) Arc voltage - Controlled arc voltage to protect semiconductor junctions; 5) Breaking capacity - Higher breaking capacity (up to 200kA) for power electronics; 6) Applications - High-speed for semiconductors, standard for general circuit protection. Standard fuses are too slow to protect expensive semiconductors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-speed fuses for semiconductor protection. Use standard/industrial fuses for general circuit protection.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Mersen fuse for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Mersen fuse involves several key steps: 1) Identify application type - semiconductor protection, DC power, or general industrial; 2) Determine voltage rating - must exceed maximum system voltage including transients; 3) Calculate current rating - typically 1.25x to 1.5x normal operating current; 4) Verify breaking capacity - must exceed maximum prospective fault current; 5) For semiconductors - match fuse I2t to semiconductor withstand capability; 6) Consider physical size and mounting type; 7) Check certification requirements for your market. LiTong provides selection guides and FAE support to help you choose the optimal fuse.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our fuse selection guides or contact our FAE team with your system parameters for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What certifications do Mersen fuses carry?
faqs.answer: Mersen fuses hold comprehensive international certifications: 1) UL Recognition - for North American market compliance; 2) cULus - for Canada and US markets; 3) IEC - International Electrotechnical Commission standards; 4) CE Marking - for European market access; 5) CCC - China Compulsory Certification; 6) VDE - German safety certification; 7) CSA - Canadian Standards Association. Specific product lines may have additional certifications for marine, railway, or hazardous location applications. All certifications are maintained through regular audits and testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify required certifications for your target market. Contact our sales team for certification documentation.

faqs.question: What industries does Mersen serve with its electrical protection solutions?
faqs.answer: Mersen provides electrical protection solutions across diverse industries: 1) Electric Vehicles - Battery protection, charging infrastructure, and powertrain systems; 2) Renewable Energy - Solar inverters, wind turbines, and energy storage systems; 3) Semiconductors - Power converters, motor drives, and UPS systems; 4) Industrial - Motor protection, power distribution, and industrial automation; 5) Data Centers - Power distribution and UPS protection; 6) Railways - Traction systems and auxiliary power; 7) Marine - Shipboard electrical systems. Each industry has specific requirements that Mersen addresses with specialized product lines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your industry and application requirements, then explore our industry-specific solutions or contact our FAE team.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Mersen fuse products?
faqs.answer: Lead times for Mersen products vary by product type and location: 1) Standard high-speed fuses - 4-6 weeks from European factories; 2) DC fuses for EV/energy storage - 6-8 weeks; 3) Industrial fuses - 2-4 weeks for standard ratings; 4) Surge protection devices - 3-5 weeks; 5) Custom or special ratings - 8-12 weeks. LiTong maintains strategic inventory of popular Mersen fuse series in Asia to support faster delivery for urgent requirements. Large volume orders may require production scheduling. Contact our sales team for current lead times on specific part numbers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current stock status and lead times. Consider forecast agreements for high-volume requirements.
